data.title: Tickets branch falls out of sync with origin during brainstorm session
data.description:
## Summary

During a brainstorm session that created 4 epics and 3 dependency links, the tickets branch accumulated 3 local-ahead commits that were never pushed to origin. Meanwhile, origin accumulated 19 commits (suggestions, other activity) that were never pulled locally. The ticket system's write_commit_event() has best-effort push logic (3-retry fetch-rebase), but it clearly failed silently during this session.

## Reproduction

1. Run a brainstorm session that creates multiple epics and links
2. After session, check sync status: git rev-list origin/tickets..tickets --count
3. Observe local-ahead commits that were never pushed

## Expected Behavior

After each ticket mutation (create, link, edit, comment), the best-effort push in write_commit_event() should sync local changes to origin. When it fails, a visible warning should be emitted so the user or orchestrator can retry.

## Actual Behavior

3 link commits accumulated locally without being pushed. No warning was visible during the session. The 19 remote-ahead commits were also not pulled, suggesting the fetch-rebase-push retry logic either didn't run or failed silently on all 3 retries.

## Impact

Tickets created during a session are invisible to other sessions, CI, and the Jira bridge until manually synced. In a multi-session or multi-worktree environment, this causes stale ticket state and potential duplicate work.
